refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation communication quality such that packet loss does not exceed the customer-defined limit under agreed network conditions.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR clarifies the performance metric (packet loss) and ties it explicitly to customer-defined limits and network conditions, ensuring measurable and testable requirements.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure packet loss percentage during teleoperation sessions under defined network conditions and verify it remains within the customer-defined limit.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report packet loss status to relevant modules for corrective action.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement adaptive communication protocol with error correction and retransmission.
DP2: Use dedicated network link with QoS prioritization for teleoperation data.
DP3: Real-time packet loss monitoring and dynamic adjustment of transmission parameters.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement adaptive communication protocol with error correction and retransmission.

potential_sources: Dependency on network link quality and telemetry data flow.
Interaction with safety services for alerting on communication degradation.
coupling_check.notes: DP1 logically isolates communication quality management within the teleoperation protocol, minimizing cross-domain coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions are within agreed parameters.
Risk of increased latency due to retransmissions affecting teleoperation responsiveness.
Assumes error correction algorithms are compatible with vehicle and teleoperation modules.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating real-time monitoring (DP3) as a complementary DP to enhance responsiveness.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for teleoperation control, communication protocol implementation, and packet loss management.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface for sending and receiving teleoperation data packets over the network.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Interface to send communication quality metrics and status to the NGHE Solution and Safety Services.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Interface to notify Safety Services of communication quality degradation or packet loss breaches.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment as communication protocol and packet loss management logically belong here; low diffusion risk.
Moderate containment if communication quality managed at system level; higher diffusion risk due to broader scope.
Low containment; vehicle module focuses on physical control, not communication protocol.
Minimal containment; safety services consume alerts but do not manage communication quality.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Packet loss management is best contained within the Teleoperation module to centralize communication protocol logic and minimize cross-module diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on Network Link interface stability.
Risk of delayed alerts if communication quality monitoring is not tightly integrated with Safety Services.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Ensure robust interface definitions and monitoring hooks between Teleoperation and Safety Services to mitigate alert latency.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (adaptive communication protocol) directly addresses the refined FR by targeting packet loss reduction with measurable mechanisms.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk as DP is well contained within Teleoperation module.
Potential interface complexity with Safety Services for alerts.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support Teleoperation as the best IM boundary for the DP.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by monitoring packet loss metrics via Teleoperation interfaces and cross-checking with SafetyAlert notifications.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibility to Teleoperation module with defined external interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Add complementary DP for real-time monitoring to improve responsiveness.
Strengthen interface protocols between Teleoperation and Safety Services.

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition sufficiently refines the FR, selects a viable DP, and assigns a clear IM boundary with manageable risks and verification paths.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report packet loss status to relevant modules for corrective action.

revise.why: No immediate revision needed; next step is to decompose sub-FRs for further detail.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Error correction and retransmission do not introduce unacceptable latency.
open_risks: Potential latency impact on teleoperation responsiveness.
Interface robustness between Teleoperation and Safety Services.
evidence_needed: Performance data on packet loss under various network conditions.
Verification of alert timeliness and accuracy through SafetyAlert interface.
